# Enrico Piovesan

> Platform Software Architect at Autodesk. Author of Universal Microservices Architecture. Builder of UMA, C-DAD, and Traverse — frameworks for portable, contract-driven, AI-navigable software systems. Based in Golden, BC, Canada.

## Core Frameworks

- **UMA (Universal Microservices Architecture)**: portable, contract-driven execution model separating business logic from runtime environment. Introduced August 2024. Book published on Amazon.
- **C-DAD (Contract-Driven AI Development)**: foundation for AI-native systems where contracts, not code, define collaboration between humans, machines, and runtimes. Introduced November 2025.
- **ECCA (Event Contract Catalog Architecture)**: governance pattern for event-driven architectures at scale. August 2025.
- **Traverse**: open source contract-driven Rust/WASM runtime for discovering, validating, and composing portable business capabilities.

## Books

- *Universal Microservices Architecture: Device-Independent Modelling for Modern Software Design with WASM* (published) — https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0GTTTTQH4
- *The Day After: How to Restructure Your Software Company for the Age of AI Agents* (forthcoming)

## White Papers (2023–2025)

- Contract-Driven AI Development (C-DAD) — November 2025
- Event Contract Catalog Architecture (ECCA) — August 2025
- Designing Adaptive AI Systems with UMA and MCP — June 2025
- Universal Microservices Architecture (UMA) — August 2024
- Client-Side Microservices Architecture (CSMA) — June 2023

## Writing

- Medium: 2,600 followers. Weekly since May 2025.
- *Mastering Software Architecture for the AI Era* (Wednesdays) — https://medium.com/software-architecture-in-the-age-of-ai
- *The Rise of Device-Independent Architecture* (Fridays) — https://medium.com/the-rise-of-device-independent-architecture

## Speaking Topics

- Write Once, Run Where It Makes Sense (Architecture / WASM)
- Contract-Driven AI Development (AI Engineering)
- The Day After: Restructuring Software for the Age of AI Agents (Engineering Leadership)

## Key Topics

WebAssembly, WASM, portable systems, AI-native architecture, microservices, contract-driven development, platform architecture, device-independent software, AI agents
